compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Epic AC7 specifies the enforcement merge-gate as `rebar verify-identity
--require-authenticated --since <identity.enforce_since>` that (a) exits non-zero when any
enforced event is not `certified`, (b) emits one structured report entry
`{event_uuid, ticket_id, commit, author, verdict, display}` per non-certified event, (c)
**grandfathers** pre-cutover events, and (d) runs as a CI merge-gate workflow. The delivered
gate (3183 / change 580) meets the SPIRIT (`rebar verify-authorship`, opt-in via config,
non-zero exit, replay-never-rejects, fsck/show grouping) but not the LETTER: wrong command
name, no `--require-authenticated`/`--since` flags, text-only output (no JSON report), no
`enforce_since` grandfathering, and no CI workflow file. The epic completion-verifier flagged
AC7 unmet. This story builds AC7 in full. (Remediation of epic gnu-whale-ichor; user chose
"build it".)

## What
Build the AC7 enforcement surface on top of the delivered merge-gate, in
`src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` (+ config + a report schema + a CI workflow):

- **`rebar verify-identity` command.** Make `verify-identity` the canonical command name
  (add it to the CLI intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`), keeping `verify-authorship`
  as a back-compat alias routing to the same `cli()`. Regenerate the CLI reference
  (`docs/cli-reference.md`) + INTERCEPT_COMMANDS/_LADDER so the drift gate stays green.
- **`--require-authenticated` flag.** Forces enforcement ON for the run regardless of
  `identity.require_authenticated` config (the CI job passes it explicitly). Without it,
  config governs (unchanged).
- **`--since <ref>` grandfathering.** Default from a new `identity.enforce_since` config
  key (env `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE`). An event is ENFORCED iff its introducing commit
  is `<ref>` or a descendant of `<ref>` (at/after cutover, via `git merge-base
  --is-ancestor <ref> <event_commit>`); an event whose commit does NOT descend `<ref>` is
  **grandfathered** — its verdict is still computed and reported, but it never fails the
  gate. With no `--since`/`enforce_since`, all events are enforced (current behavior).
- **`--format json` report.** Emit a JSON array of entries
  `{event_uuid, ticket_id, commit, author, verdict, display}` — one per NON-`verified`
  in-scope event (with a `grandfathered: bool`), validated against a new
  `src/rebar/schemas/verify_identity_report.schema.json`. `display` is the grouping
  (`verified`/`unverified`/`unsigned`); `verdict` is the raw verdict. Default output stays
  the existing human text.
- **CI merge-gate workflow.** Add `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` (or a gated step)
  that runs `rebar verify-identity --require-authenticated --since <identity.enforce_since>`
  over the merged log and fails the build on a non-zero exit.
- Exit semantics: non-zero iff an ENFORCED (non-grandfathered) event is not `verified`
  (`certified`), under `--require-authenticated` (or config). Grandfathered/advisory → 0.

## Scope
In: the `verify-identity` command name + alias, `--require-authenticated`/`--since` flags,
`enforce_since` config + env, the JSON report + its schema, grandfathering by commit
ancestry, and the CI workflow. Out: the per-event classification itself (delivered +
TS2/TS3, unchanged) and the schema verdict enum (AC10). Folds into the 3183 commit (change
580); the CI workflow + report schema are new files in that commit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar verify-identity --all` behaves as the merge-gate (same classification), and
  `rebar verify-authorship --all` still works as an alias (test both dispatch).
- [ ] `--require-authenticated` forces enforcement: with config off, `rebar verify-identity
  --require-authenticated --all` still exits non-zero on an unsigned/non-verified enforced
  event (test asserts exit code).
- [ ] `--since <ref>` grandfathers pre-cutover events: with an unsigned event introduced
  BEFORE the cutover ref and another AFTER, `--require-authenticated --since <ref>` fails
  ONLY for the post-cutover one; the pre-cutover event is reported `grandfathered` and does
  NOT fail the gate (test asserts exit code + that the pre-cutover event is grandfathered).
- [ ] `--format json` emits an array of `{event_uuid, ticket_id, commit, author, verdict,
  display, grandfathered}` entries for the non-verified events, validating against
  `verify_identity_report.schema.json`; a test asserts an entry's field set + that `display`
  is the grouping value and `verdict` the raw verdict.
- [ ] A CI workflow file exists under `.github/workflows/` that invokes `rebar
  verify-identity --require-authenticated --since <…>` as a gating job (test/asserts the
  file exists and references the command).
- [ ] The CLI-reference drift gate passes (verify-identity documented; alias noted).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` (verify-identity name + --require-authenticated/--since flags + JSON report + grandfathering)
- `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (intercept verify-identity + alias verify-authorship)
- `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` (identity.enforce_since + env REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE)
- `src/rebar/schemas/verify_identity_report.schema.json` (new report schema)
- `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` (new CI merge-gate workflow)
- `docs/cli-reference.md` (regenerated: verify-identity)
compiled_state.title: identity: verify-identity enforcement CLI + --since grandfathering + JSON report + CI gate (epic AC7)
